role: Core documentation and governance artifacts defining the ArchSpine mirror system's identity, rules, and configuration.
responsibility: These documents collectively establish the ArchSpine system as a centralized architectural governance layer—they articulate its purpose, define its configuration schema and validation rules, outline its maintenance scope and invariants, and provide narrative context for understanding how the mirror synchronizes with the codebase to enforce architectural rules, generate views, and maintain documentation.

children.role: Documentation and governance mirror system for software architecture
Project overview and introduction to the ArchSpine mirror system

Defines the purpose, scope, and responsibilities of the ArchSpine mirror system—a centralized governance layer that captures, maintains, and evolves architectural documentation and rules for a software project.

System overview document for ArchSpine, a documentation mirror and governance tool that synchronizes a structured atlas of documentation with the codebase, enabling automated consistency checks and knowledge views.
Defines the core identity, boundaries, and governance contract of the ArchSpine mirror system.
